metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Electronics and Robotics 10 teaches students about electrical circuits, electronic components, and robotics systems. Students design and build circuits, learn about microcontrollers and programming, and create robotic systems that solve problems. Through hands-on projects, they explore how electronics and automation work while developing troubleshooting and design skills.
metadata.general_description: Elective course focused on circuits, electronics, microcontrollers, and robotics.

big_ideas.statement: Circuits and electrical systems follow predictable rules that can be understood and harnessed to create function.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn that electricity, resistance, current, and voltage follow laws that allow designers to predict and control circuit behavior. Understanding these principles enables creation of reliable electronic systems.

big_ideas.statement: Microcontrollers and programming enable electronic systems to sense, think, and respond to their environment.
big_ideas.elaboration: By combining circuits with programming, students create systems that are intelligent and responsive. This integration of hardware and software is the foundation of modern electronics.

big_ideas.statement: Robotics applies electronics, mechanics, and programming to create systems that perform useful work.
big_ideas.elaboration: Robots combine multiple technologies to achieve goals. Students learn how mechanical design, electronics, sensors, and code work together to create autonomous or controlled systems.

big_ideas.statement: Electronics and robotics solve real problems but also raise questions about automation, displacement, and ethics.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop awareness that these technologies have benefits and challenges. Critical thinking about how technology is used and its impact on people is essential.

i_can_statements: I can design circuits that meet specific requirements
I can select appropriate components for designs
I can plan circuits with safety in mind
I can evaluate and improve circuit designs
I can identify problems that robots can solve
I can design mechanical and electrical systems for robots
I can plan robot behavior and programming
I can prototype and iterate on robotic designs
I can select and use appropriate sensors
I can design control systems and feedback loops
I can plan autonomous vs. controlled operation
I can design user interfaces for systems
I can create circuit diagrams
I can document system designs
I can explain system operation clearly
I can present designs and results
I can assemble circuits on breadboards and PCBs
I can solder electronic components
I can test circuits with multimeters and tools
I can troubleshoot circuit problems
I can write code to control electronic systems
I can use sensor data in programs
I can debug and test code
I can program complex behaviors
I can assemble robotic platforms and systems
I can integrate mechanical and electrical components
I can program robots to perform tasks
I can tune and optimize robot performance
I can organize and manage components
I can work safely with electronics
I can manage complex multi-component projects
I can maintain and repair systems
I can explain voltage, current, and resistance
I can apply Ohm's law and circuit analysis
I can understand AC and DC circuits
I can calculate power and energy
I can identify and select resistors, capacitors, diodes
I can understand transistors and integrated circuits
I can work with sensors and actuators
I can understand component specifications
I can use breadboards, oscilloscopes, and multimeters
I can solder safely and effectively
I can use PCB design software
I can program with various platforms
I can understand advanced sensor systems
I can explore machine learning and AI in robotics
I can understand industrial automation
I can explore emerging robotics technologies
